title: echium rosulatum subsp. rosulatum
intro: Echium rosulatum subsp. rosulatum, also known as the Portuguese bugloss, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the Iberian Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which have been found to have potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting off infections. The health benefits of Echium rosulatum subsp. rosulatum include its potential to alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its use in treating skin conditions like eczema and acne. Echium rosulatum subsp. rosulatum can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and reap its potential health benefits.
